script:
#!/bin/bash
set +e
echo '========================================'
echo 'PHASE 4B: FIX REMAINING EXPOSED PORTS'
echo '========================================'

# --- Find the actual WP compose location ---
echo '--- Finding WP compose ---'
find / -maxdepth 6 -name 'docker-compose*.yml' 2>/dev/null | while read f; do
  if grep -ql 'mebelit\|wordpress' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "FOUND WP compose: $f"
  fi
done

# Check if mebelit containers have a compose file embedded
echo ''
echo '--- Docker compose project details ---'
docker inspect mebelit-wp-db-1 --format '{{index .Config.Labels "com.docker.compose.project.config_files"}}' 2>/dev/null
docker inspect mebelit-wp-db-1 --format '{{index .Config.Labels "com.docker.compose.project.working_dir"}}' 2>/dev/null

# The compose file is gone. Use DOCKER-USER iptables chain to block external access.
echo ''
echo '--- Blocking MySQL 3306 and WP 8080 via iptables DOCKER-USER chain ---'

# DOCKER-USER chain is the correct way to filter Docker traffic
# Block external access to 3306 (allow only from 127.0.0.0/8 and Docker networks 172.16.0.0/12)
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null || \
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null || \
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -j DROP 2>/dev/null || \
  iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -j DROP

# Block external access to 8080
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null || \
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null || \
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP 2>/dev/null || \
  iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP

echo 'iptables DOCKER-USER rules added for 3306 and 8080'
echo ''
echo '--- DOCKER-USER chain ---'
iptables -L DOCKER-USER -n -v

# --- Fix vk-callback to bind to 127.0.0.1 ---
echo ''
echo '--- Fixing vk-callback (port 3102) ---'
# Check the server.js for listen call
grep -n 'listen' /opt/vk-callback/server.js 2>/dev/null
echo ''
# Modify to bind to 127.0.0.1
if grep -q "listen(.*3102" /opt/vk-callback/server.js 2>/dev/null; then
  cp /opt/vk-callback/server.js /opt/vk-callback/server.js.backup
  # Replace listen(PORT) or listen(3102) with listen(PORT, '127.0.0.1')
  sed -i "s/\.listen(PORT)/\.listen(PORT, '127.0.0.1')/g" /opt/vk-callback/server.js
  sed -i "s/\.listen(3102)/\.listen(3102, '127.0.0.1')/g" /opt/vk-callback/server.js
  systemctl restart vk-callback
  echo 'vk-callback restarted on 127.0.0.1'
else
  # Try alternative: use HOST env
  echo 'Trying HOST=127.0.0.1 in service'
  SVC='/etc/systemd/system/vk-callback.service'
  if grep -q 'Environment=HOST' "$SVC" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo 'HOST already set'
  else
    sed -i '/\[Service\]/a Environment=HOST=127.0.0.1' "$SVC" 2>/dev/null
    systemctl daemon-reload
    systemctl restart vk-callback
    echo 'vk-callback restarted with HOST=127.0.0.1'
  fi
fi

# Also block 3102 via iptables as safety net
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3102 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null || \
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3102 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3102 -j DROP 2>/dev/null || \
  iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3102 -j DROP

# Make iptables rules persistent
echo ''
echo '--- Making iptables persistent ---'
apt-get install -y iptables-persistent 2>/dev/null | tail -3
netfilter-persistent save 2>/dev/null || iptables-save > /etc/iptables/rules.v4 2>/dev/null
echo 'iptables rules saved'

# --- Persist upload_server not to restart ---
echo ''
echo '--- Ensuring upload_server stays down ---'
# Check if it was started by cron or something
crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -i upload

echo ''
echo '========================================'
echo 'PHASE 4B COMPLETE'
echo '========================================'
intent: Phase 4B: Block MySQL/WP via iptables DOCKER-USER, fix vk-callback, persist rules
